0-1-2-5-8_b0.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-2-5-8_b0.note: Fm6‚ô≠13/C is preferred because F-A‚ô≠-C-D forms a complete F minor-sixth sonority with C as the fifth in the bass, while D‚ô≠ is explainable as flat-thirteen color rather than a chord-defining major seventh against D. Music21's Dm7‚ô≠5/C and tonal's D‚ô≠maj7‚ô≠9/C are both structurally plausible re-roots, so this remains a genuine ambiguity, but the C bass gives the F-rooted reading a conventional inversion advantage.

0-1-2-4-6-9-10_b2.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-9-10_b2.note: F‚ôØ7(‚ôØ9,‚ôØ11,‚ô≠13)/D is preferred because the voicing contains the complete F‚ôØ altered-dominant shell F‚ôØ-A‚ôØ-C‚ôØ-E with D as flat thirteenth and A/GùÑ™ as sharp-nine color. Tonal agrees as F#7#9#11b13/D, and ChoCo common-name counts favor dominant7 sharp-nine sharp-eleven flat-thirteen signatures over augmented-major-nine variants. Music21's B‚ô≠aug(maj9)/D add D‚ô≠,E is a related add-tone spelling, but it misses the complete dominant organization.

0-1-2-4-6-9-10_b4.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-9-10_b4.note: F‚ôØ7(‚ôØ9,‚ôØ11,‚ô≠13)/E is preferred because E is the flat seventh of a complete F‚ôØ altered dominant with sharp-nine, sharp-eleven, and flat-thirteen color. Tonal agrees. Music21's B‚ô≠aug(maj9)/E add C‚ôØ preserves an augmented-major upper structure, but it demotes the dominant seventh and altered-dominant function to added tones.

0-1-2-4-6-9-10_b9.label: oracle-limitation

0-1-2-4-6-7-10_b0.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-2-4-6-7-10_b0.note: F‚ôØ7(‚ô≠9,‚ôØ11,‚ô≠13)/B‚ôØ is preferred because the voicing contains the complete F‚ôØ altered-dominant shell F‚ôØ-A‚ôØ-C‚ôØ-E with G as flat ninth, C/B‚ôØ as sharp eleventh, and D as flat thirteenth. Tonal agrees. Music21's C9(‚ô≠9,‚ôØ11) has a plausible root-position C bass, so this remains a genuine tritone-dominant ambiguity, but it requires simultaneous flat and natural ninths against C while the F‚ôØ reading names the full altered stack directly.

0-1-2-4-6-7-10_b1.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-7-10_b1.note: F‚ôØ7(‚ô≠9,‚ôØ11,‚ô≠13)/C‚ôØ is preferred because C‚ôØ is the fifth of a complete F‚ôØ altered dominant, and every sounding pitch is explained as shell tone or standard altered-dominant color. 0-1-2-4-6-7-10_b2.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-7-10_b2.note: F‚ôØ7(‚ô≠9,‚ôØ11,‚ô≠13)/D is preferred because D is the flat thirteenth of the complete F‚ôØ altered-dominant stack. Tonal agrees. Music21's C9/D add-tone spelling keeps a C dominant shell but treats the altered colors as additions and loses the direct F‚ôØ dominant function.

0-1-2-4-6-7-10_b4.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-7-10_b4.note: F‚ôØ7(‚ô≠9,‚ôØ11,‚ô≠13)/E is preferred because E is the flat seventh of a complete F‚ôØ altered dominant with flat-nine, sharp-eleven, and flat-thirteen color. Tonal agrees. 0-1-2-4-6-7-10_b7.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-7-10_b7.note: F‚ôØ7(‚ô≠9,‚ôØ11,‚ô≠13)/G is preferred because G is the flat ninth of the complete F‚ôØ altered-dominant stack. Tonal agrees. 0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b0.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b0.note: F‚ôØ9(‚ôØ11,‚ô≠13)/B‚ôØ is preferred because the voicing contains the complete F‚ôØ dominant stack F‚ôØ-A‚ôØ-C‚ôØ-E plus G‚ôØ, B‚ôØ, and D. Tonal agrees with F#9#11b13/C. 0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b1.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b1.note: F‚ôØ9(‚ôØ11,‚ô≠13)/C‚ôØ is preferred because C‚ôØ is the fifth of the complete F‚ôØ9‚ôØ11‚ô≠13 stack. Tonal agrees. Music21's C‚ôØm(maj7) add F‚ôØ,D,A‚ôØ re-roots the same pitch material away from the complete dominant shell.

0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b2.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b2.note: F‚ôØ9(‚ôØ11,‚ô≠13)/D is preferred because D is the flat thirteenth of a complete F‚ôØ Lydian-dominant-flat-thirteen sonority. Tonal agrees. Music21's C9‚ôØ5/D add C‚ôØ,F‚ôØ preserves some altered-dominant color but misses the complete F‚ôØ dominant organization.

0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b4.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b4.note: F‚ôØ9(‚ôØ11,‚ô≠13)/E is preferred because E is the dominant seventh of the complete F‚ôØ9‚ôØ11‚ô≠13 stack. Tonal agrees. Music21's E9‚ôØ5 add C‚ôØ,A‚ôØ is a related altered-fifth add-tone spelling, but it treats F‚ôØ as the ninth of E rather than the clearer dominant root.

0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b8.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b8.note: F‚ôØ9(‚ôØ11,‚ô≠13)/G‚ôØ is preferred because G‚ôØ is the ninth of the complete F‚ôØ9‚ôØ11‚ô≠13 stack. Tonal agrees. Music21's G‚ôØ7‚ô≠5 add E,A‚ôØ,C‚ôØ describes related pitch classes but loses the complete F‚ôØ dominant shell.

0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b10.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b10.note: F‚ôØ9(‚ôØ11,‚ô≠13)/A‚ôØ is preferred because A‚ôØ is the major third of the complete F‚ôØ9‚ôØ11‚ô≠13 stack. Tonal agrees. Music21's A‚ô≠9‚ôØ5/B‚ô≠ add D,D‚ô≠ is an enharmonic altered-fifth bookkeeping label rather than the direct Lydian-dominant-flat-thirteen reading.

0-1-2-5-8_b5.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-2-5-8_b5.note: Fm6‚ô≠13 is preferred because F-A‚ô≠-C-D forms a complete root-position F minor-sixth chord, with D‚ô≠ acting as flat-thirteen or lower-neighbor color. Music21's Dm7‚ô≠5/F is the enharmonic half-diminished counterpart and tonal's D‚ô≠maj7‚ô≠9/F is also complete but inverted and requires EùÑ´ for its flat ninth. 0-1-3-5-8-9_b8.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-5-8-9_b8.note: F7(‚ôØ9,‚ô≠13)/G‚ôØ is preferred because F, A, C, and E‚ô≠ form a complete dominant seventh, with G‚ôØ/A‚ô≠ as sharp-nine bass color and D‚ô≠ as flat thirteenth. Tonal agrees as F7#9b13/Ab. Music21's A augmented-major-seventh add-tone spelling names a plausible upper structure but re-roots away from the complete altered-dominant shell that musicians would usually treat as primary.

0-1-3-5-6-9_b3.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-5-6-9_b3.note: F7(‚ô≠9,‚ô≠13)/E‚ô≠ is preferred because E‚ô≠ is the flat seventh of a complete F altered dominant. Tonal agrees. Music21's D‚ô≠ augmented-major-eleventh slash label captures a related upper structure, but it re-roots away from the complete dominant shell and is less useful as the primary chord symbol.

0-1-3-5-7_b5.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-5-7_b5.note: C‚ôØmaj9‚ô≠5/E‚ôØ is preferred because C‚ôØ-E‚ôØ-G-B‚ôØ-D‚ôØ forms a complete major-seventh-flat-five ninth sonority, with E‚ôØ as the major third in the bass and every sounding pitch named directly. Tonal agrees as D‚ô≠M9‚ô≠5/F. Music21's Cm/F add D‚ô≠ keeps a C-minor shell but treats F and D‚ô≠ as additions, missing the complete C‚ôØ/D‚ô≠ major-seventh-flat-five organization.

0-1-3-5-7_b7.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-5-7_b7.note: C‚ôØmaj9‚ô≠5/G is preferred because G is the flat fifth of the complete C‚ôØmaj9‚ô≠5 structure, while C, E‚ôØ/F, and D‚ôØ/E‚ô≠ supply the major seventh, third, and ninth. Tonal agrees as D‚ô≠M9‚ô≠5/G. Music21's Cm/G add F,D‚ô≠ describes a related C-minor shell but demotes the major-third role and is less complete as a musician-facing chord symbol.

0-1-3-5-7-9_b0.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-3-5-7-9_b0.note: F9‚ô≠13/C is preferred because F-A-C-E‚ô≠-G-D‚ô≠ forms a complete dominant nine flat-thirteen stack with C as the fifth in the bass. Music21 supports the F root but treats D‚ô≠ as added sharp-five color, while tonal's E‚ô≠13‚ô≠5/C is also a complete Lydian-dominant organization. The stronger F dominant shell and stable fifth bass make WhatChord's label defensible, but the E‚ô≠ reading is a real competing analysis.

0-1-3-5-7-9_b5.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-3-5-7-9_b5.note: F9‚ô≠13 is preferred because the voicing is a complete root-position F dominant with ninth and flat-thirteenth color. Music21 re-roots the same pitch classes as a D‚ô≠ augmented-major ninth add-tone spelling, and tonal chooses the complete E‚ô≠13‚ô≠5/F Lydian-dominant reading. The F bass makes the F9‚ô≠13 label the clearest primary symbol, while the other complete structures keep this as genuine ambiguity.

0-1-3-5-7-9_b7.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-3-5-7-9_b7.note: F9‚ô≠13/G is preferred because it preserves the complete F-A-C-E‚ô≠ dominant shell with G as the ninth in the bass and D‚ô≠ as flat thirteenth. Music21 also keeps the F root, though it spells D‚ô≠ as added sharp-five color. Tonal's E‚ô≠13‚ô≠5/G gives the bass a stronger third role and is a conventional alternate, so the row is genuinely ambiguous rather than an oracle error.

0-1-3-5-7-9_b9.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-3-5-7-9_b9.note: F9‚ô≠13/A is preferred because A is the major third of the complete F9‚ô≠13 stack, giving the F-rooted dominant a conventional first-inversion bass role. Music21 supports the F root with an added-sharp-five spelling, while tonal's E‚ô≠13‚ô≠5/A is a complete alternate with A as the flat fifth. The stable F-dominant inversion is the clearer primary label, but both dominant organizations are defensible.

0-1-3-6-9_b9.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-3-6-9_b9.note: D#m7b5(add13)/A is defensible; the set is the symmetric dim7 {C,Eb,Gb,A} plus the chromatic Db, so root assignment is inherently ambiguous and every source picks a different root (whatchord D#, music21 F#, tonal Db). It is not a functional dominant: the notes are a subset of the C half-whole octatonic but lack any 3rd and b7, so no tritone. Our #1 is a complete m7b5 with a natural 13 and the bass (b5) as a core tone, and wins a 0.10 near-tie over Cdim7(b9) via natural-extension-over-altered-color. music21's F#o7/AaddC# is enharmonically identical to our F#m6#11/A reading (same root, intervals {0,3,6,7,9}, bass A), but we rank that #3 at delta 0.22, below the near-tie cutoff, so it does not surface; among the readings the app actually shows (#1 and the #2 Cdim7(b9) near-tie) no oracle agrees. tonal roots on Db, matching nothing of ours. The intuitive bass-rooted Adim7+chromatic description ranks low because Db will not sit cleanly on Adim7. Genuine ambiguity; no engine change warranted.

0-1-3-6-9_b1.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-3-6-9_b1.note: D‚ôØm7‚ô≠5(add13)/C‚ôØ is defensible because D‚ôØ-F‚ôØ-A-C‚ôØ forms a complete half-diminished seventh shell with C/B‚ôØ as natural-thirteenth color and C‚ôØ as the flat seventh in the bass. The pitch set is a symmetric diminished-seventh core plus one chromatic tone, so root assignment is inherently ambiguous: music21's F‚ôØm/C‚ôØ add E‚ô≠,C matches the surfaced F‚ôØm6‚ôØ11/C‚ôØ alternate, tonal's D‚ô≠maj9‚ôØ5sus4 gives a bass-rooted suspended major-seventh view, and Cdim7‚ô≠9/D‚ô≠ is also a near-tie. The complete half-diminished shell and conventional seventh bass make WhatChord's primary label defensible, but the competing organizations are musically real.

0-1-3-6-9_b6.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-3-6-9_b6.note: D‚ôØm7‚ô≠5(add13)/F‚ôØ is defensible because F‚ôØ is the minor third of the complete D‚ôØ half-diminished-seventh shell D‚ôØ-F‚ôØ-A-C‚ôØ, with C/B‚ôØ as natural-thirteenth color. The same notes also support music21's D‚ôØdim7/F‚ôØ add C‚ôØ view, the near-tie Cdim7‚ô≠9/G‚ô≠ reading, and tonal's D‚ô≠maj9‚ôØ5sus4/F‚ôØ suspended-major re-rooting. Because the voicing is a diminished-seventh core plus one chromatic tone, no single root is forced; the half-diminished inversion is a clear primary label, but the row is genuinely ambiguous.

0-1-3-8_b1.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-3-8_b1.note: D‚ô≠maj7sus2 is correct, and stronger than the b0 sibling: with D‚ô≠ in the bass it is a complete root-position reading (all 4 notes as core template tones, bass = root, every interval consonant), so it wins on structural fit beyond the tie-break window. Ab/Db (music21, pychord) roots on the complete Ab triad but strands D‚ô≠ as a natural 11, the avoid-note minor 9th against the major third (C); that C/D‚ô≠ minor-9th pair is better read as root+maj7 (our D‚ô≠) than as two non-root color tones. tonal also re-roots away from Ab (Cm(b6,b9)/Db), so the split is triad-first matchers vs harmonic reasoners, not a true Ab consensus; ours and tonal agree on rejecting Ab as root. Genuine ambiguity; sound; no engine change needed.

0-1-3-4-6-7-10_b3.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-3-4-6-7-10_b3.note: E‚ô≠13(‚ô≠9,‚ôØ9) is preferred because E‚ô≠ is the bass/root and the voicing contains the complete E‚ô≠ dominant shell E‚ô≠-G-B‚ô≠-D‚ô≠, with C as the thirteenth and F‚ô≠/E plus F‚ôØ/G‚ô≠ as split flat-nine/sharp-nine color. Tonal's F‚ôØ13‚ô≠9‚ôØ11/E‚ô≠ is also a complete altered dominant organization of the same pitch classes, so the alternate is real; the root-position E‚ô≠ bass makes WhatChord's label the more direct context-free name.

0-1-3-5-7-9_b3.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-5-7-9_b3.note: E‚ô≠13‚ôØ11 is preferred because E‚ô≠, G, D‚ô≠, F, A, and C form a root-position Lydian-dominant thirteenth stack. Tonal agrees on the E‚ô≠ dominant root but normalizes the tritone as flat five rather than sharp eleven; WhatChord's sharp-eleven spelling is clearer with the ninth and thirteenth present. Music21's D‚ô≠aug(maj9)/E‚ô≠ add G is an upper-structure add-tone spelling that misses the complete dominant organization.

0-1-3-5-7-10_b7.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-5-7-10_b7.note: E‚ô≠13/G is preferred because the bass is the dominant third and the voicing contains the complete E‚ô≠ dominant thirteenth stack. Tonal agrees. 0-1-3-5-7-10_b10.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-5-7-10_b10.note: E‚ô≠13/B‚ô≠ is preferred because the bass is the dominant fifth and the voicing contains the complete E‚ô≠ dominant thirteenth stack. Tonal agrees. Music21's Cm7/B‚ô≠ add F,D‚ô≠ describes related upper-structure material but misses the more direct dominant-root organization.

0-1-4-6-10_b0.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-6-10_b0.note: C7‚ô≠5‚ô≠9 is preferred because C is the bass/root of a complete altered dominant containing C, E, G‚ô≠, B‚ô≠, and D‚ô≠. Music21 and tonal both support the tritone-related F‚ôØ7‚ôØ11/C reading, which is also complete and conventional, and ChoCo shows comparable support for the broad altered-dominant families. The root-position C bass makes WhatChord's primary label defensible, but the tritone-substitute reading keeps this a genuine ambiguity.

0-1-2-4-6-9-10_b0.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-2-4-6-9-10_b0.note: C13(‚ô≠9,‚ôØ11) is preferred because C is the bass/root and the voicing contains the complete C dominant shell C-E-B‚ô≠ with D‚ô≠, D, F‚ôØ, and A as altered and extended dominant color. Tonal's F‚ôØ7‚ôØ9‚ôØ11‚ô≠13/C is also a complete tritone-related dominant, and ChoCo has comparable support for the broad C Lydian-dominant-thirteenth family and the F‚ôØ sharp-nine sharp-eleven flat-thirteen family. The C bass makes the root-position label defensible, but this is a genuine tritone-dominant ambiguity.

0-1-4-6-10_b4.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-6-10_b4.note: C7‚ô≠5‚ô≠9/E is preferred because it is a complete altered dominant with its major third in the bass, while F‚ôØ7‚ôØ11/E is the complete tritone-related dominant in third inversion. Music21 and tonal choose the valid F‚ôØ reading, but the two chord signatures are similarly common in ChoCo and the E bass gives the C-rooted interpretation the more conventional inversion. 0-1-4-6-8-10_b0.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-6-8-10_b0.note: C7#5(b9,#11) is preferred because it names the complete C altered dominant C-E-Bb plus Db, F#, and G#/Ab. 0-1-4-6-8-10_b1.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-6-8-10_b1.note: C7#5(b9,#11)/Db is preferred because the pitch set is the complete C altered dominant with Db as flat-nine in the bass. Tonal agrees and also lists F#9#11/Db; music21 prefers the same valid tritone-related F# structure. This is a genuine dominant-function ambiguity, with WhatChord choosing the direct altered-dominant spelling.

0-1-4-6-8-10_b4.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-6-8-10_b4.note: C7#5(b9,#11)/E is preferred because E is the major third of the complete C altered dominant, while F#9#11/E is the valid tritone-related dominant with its flat seventh in the bass. Tonal agrees with the C reading and lists F#9#11/E as the alternate; music21 prefers the F# label, confirming a genuine split rather than a clear oracle winner.

0-1-4-6-8-10_b8.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-6-8-10_b8.note: C7#5(b9,#11)/G# is preferred because G#/Ab is the sharp fifth of the complete C altered dominant and avoids the less idiomatic Ab11#5 primary label. Tonal agrees with the C reading and lists F#9#11/Ab as the alternate; music21 prefers F#9/G# add B#. The remaining disagreement is a valid tritone-sub ambiguity.

0-1-4-6-8-10_b10.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-6-8-10_b10.note: C7#5(b9,#11)/Bb is preferred because Bb is the dominant seventh of the complete C altered dominant. Tonal agrees and lists the valid F#9#11/Bb alternate; music21's Gm9/Bb add C is semantically the same F#9#11 pitch-class structure. This is a genuine altered-dominant vs tritone-related split, not a reason to re-rank away from C.

0-1-4-5-8_b0.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-4-5-8_b0.note: Fm(maj7)‚ô≠13/C is preferred because the voicing cleanly contains F-A‚ô≠-C-E as a complete F minor-major seventh with D‚ô≠ as flat-thirteen color and C as the fifth in the bass. Tonal agrees as FmMaj7b6/C. Music21's D‚ô≠m(maj7)/C add F respells the same pitch set with both minor and major thirds, which is less useful as the primary musician-facing label.

0-1-4-5-8_b4.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-4-5-8_b4.note: Fm(maj7)‚ô≠13/E is preferred because E is the major seventh of the complete F-A‚ô≠-C-E shell and D‚ô≠ is conventional flat-thirteen color. Tonal agrees. Music21's C‚ôØm(maj7)/E add E‚ôØ treats F as an added major third above C‚ôØ while also keeping E as the minor third, so the split reflects oracle spelling limits rather than a stronger C‚ôØ-rooted analysis.

0-1-4-5-8_b8.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-4-5-8_b8.note: Fm(maj7)‚ô≠13/A‚ô≠ is preferred because A‚ô≠ is the minor third of a complete F minor-major seventh sonority with D‚ô≠ as flat thirteenth. Tonal agrees. Music21's C‚ôØm(maj7)/G‚ôØ add E‚ôØ preserves related pitch classes but requires simultaneous minor and major thirds, making it less idiomatic than the direct Fm(maj7)‚ô≠13 reading.

0-1-4-7_b1.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-7_b1.note: D‚ô≠m(maj7)‚ôØ11 is preferred because D‚ô≠ is the bass/root and the voicing contains the complete D‚ô≠-F‚ô≠-A‚ô≠-C minor-major-seventh shell, with G as sharp-eleventh color. Music21's D‚ô≠ diminished add-tone spelling and tonal's D‚ô≠ diminished-major-seventh or C major-add-flat-nine alternatives describe related pitch-class organizations, but they are less direct musician-facing names for this root-position minor-major sonority. The close alternate structures make the reviewed split a genuine ambiguity.

0-1-4-7_b0.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-4-7_b0.note: Cadd‚ô≠9 is preferred because C-E-G is a complete root-position C major triad and D‚ô≠ is the single chromatic flat-nine color. This is the musician-facing name for the observed voicing, not plain C major. Music21's surface label CaddC# is semantically equivalent, while tonal's CMaddb9 normalization reports a major-add-flat-nine vocabulary mismatch rather than a stronger alternate root.

0-1-4-7_b4.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-4-7_b4.note: Cadd‚ô≠9/E is preferred because E is the third of a complete C major triad and D‚ô≠ remains the only added chromatic color. Music21's C/E add C# is semantically the same voicing, and tonal's CMaddb9/E agrees on the C-rooted add-flat-nine structure despite normalization differences. The E-rooted alternatives are less idiomatic because they recast the complete C triad as altered minor or diminished material.

0-1-4-7-10_b1.label: clearly-correct
0-1-4-7-10_b1.note: A‚ôØdim7(add9)/C‚ôØ is preferred because A‚ôØ-C‚ôØ-E-G is a complete diminished-seventh chord and B‚ôØ/C supplies the added ninth, with C‚ôØ as the minor third in the bass. Music21 agrees semantically. Tonal's D‚ô≠ diminished-major-seventh normalization preserves the same symmetric core but drops the added-ninth function, so it is less complete as the primary label.

0-1-4-7-10_b10.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-7-10_b10.note: A‚ôØdim7(add9) is preferred because B‚ô≠/A‚ôØ is the bass/root of a complete diminished-seventh chord with C/B‚ôØ as the added ninth. The same symmetric diminished-seventh collection also supports Gdim7(add11)/B‚ô≠ and tonal's C7‚ô≠9/B‚ô≠ interpretation, so multiple conventional names are available. The root-position diminished reading is a defensible primary label, while the dominant-flat-nine reading remains a valid alternate rather than an engine problem.

0-1-4-7-8_b0.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-4-7-8_b0.note: A‚ô≠maj7‚ôØ5(add11)/C is preferred because A‚ô≠-C-E-G forms a complete augmented major-seventh shell, with D‚ô≠ as added eleventh and C as the major third in the bass. Music21's D‚ô≠m(maj7)‚ôØ11/C is a plausible re-rooting of the same pitch classes, but it treats C as a major seventh under a minor-major shell rather than preserving the more direct augmented-major organization. Tonal and pychord provide no usable competing label.

0-1-4-8_b0.label: clearly-correct
0-1-4-8_b0.note: C‚ôØm(maj7)/B‚ôØ is preferred because C‚ôØ-E-G‚ôØ-B‚ôØ forms a complete minor-major-seventh chord and the bass is the major seventh. Tonal agrees semantically as D‚ô≠m(maj7)/C. Music21's A‚ô≠ augmented add-eleven spelling and the nearby C augmented add-flat-nine alternative both describe related color, but they do not explain all four notes as core chord tones as directly.

0-1-4-9_b0.label: context-dependent
0-1-4-9_b0.note: C6‚ô≠9 is preferred in neutral/C context because C is the bass/root and the voicing directly supplies the major third, sixth, and flat-ninth color. Music21's Am/C add C‚ôØ is the split-third relative-minor view, while tonal's D‚ô≠m‚ô≠6M7/C matches the C‚ôØm(maj7)‚ô≠13/B‚ôØ alternate that becomes preferred in C‚ôØ minor. The row is context-dependent rather than an engine issue: C-bass root-position evidence favors C6‚ô≠9 here, but C‚ôØ minor context legitimately favors the minor-major-seventh reading.

0-1-4-7_b7.label: clearly-correct
0-1-4-7_b7.note: Cadd‚ô≠9/G is the clearest interpretation because it preserves a complete C-major triad in conventional second inversion and names D‚ô≠ as the single chromatic flat ninth. Music21 and tonal both agree on the C-rooted add-flat-nine structure. The E-rooted alternatives require less conventional mixed color tones, while C‚ôØdim/G drops the sounding C.

0-1-5-7-9_b7.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-5-7-9_b7.note: A7‚ôØ5‚ôØ9/G is preferred because A-C‚ôØ-E‚ôØ-G is a complete altered dominant seventh in third inversion, with C/B‚ôØ as conventional sharp-nine color. Tonal agrees. Music21's F/G add C‚ôØ keeps an F major shell but treats G as an added ninth and C‚ôØ as chromatic color, so it misses the chord-defining dominant seventh.

0-1-5-7-9_b9.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-5-7-9_b9.note: A7‚ôØ5‚ôØ9 is preferred because A is the bass/root and the voicing names every tone as root, major third, sharp fifth, flat seventh, and sharp ninth of a standard altered dominant. Tonal agrees, and ChoCo common-name counts strongly favor dominant7-sharp5 sharp-nine signatures over sparse major add-tone variants. Music21's F/A add G,C‚ôØ is an upper-structure major-triad label that drops the primary dominant identity.

0-1-5-9_b1.label: clearly-correct
0-1-5-9_b1.note: D‚ô≠maj7‚ôØ5 is preferred because D‚ô≠-F-A-B‚ôØ/C forms a complete augmented major-seventh sonority in root position. Tonal lists both D‚ô≠maj7‚ô≠13 and D‚ô≠maj7‚ôØ5, but the sharp-fifth spelling makes the altered fifth chord-defining rather than treating A as flat-thirteen color. Music21's A augmented add-sharp-nine slash spelling is a related augmented-triad view but is less direct for the root-position D‚ô≠ structure.

0-1-5-6-10_b0.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-5-6-10_b0.note: G‚ô≠maj7‚ôØ11/C is preferred because the voicing contains the complete G‚ô≠ major-seventh shell G‚ô≠-B‚ô≠-D‚ô≠-F with C as ‚ôØ11. Music21 agrees semantically. Tonal's F‚ôØmaj‚ôØ4/C keeps the Lydian color but drops the major seventh from its normalized structure, so the split reflects oracle vocabulary rather than an engine issue.

0-1-5-6-10_b1.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-5-6-10_b1.note: G‚ô≠maj7‚ôØ11/D‚ô≠ is preferred because D‚ô≠ is the fifth of a complete G‚ô≠maj7‚ôØ11 voicing. Music21 agrees semantically as F‚ôØmaj7/C‚ôØ add B‚ôØ. Tonal's F‚ôØmaj‚ôØ4/D‚ô≠ omits the major seventh in normalization, making it a less complete primary label.

0-1-5-6-10_b5.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-5-6-10_b5.note: G‚ô≠maj7‚ôØ11/F is preferred because F is the major seventh in the bass and the voicing contains root, third, fifth, major seventh, and ‚ôØ11. Music21 agrees semantically. Tonal's F‚ôØmaj‚ôØ4/F captures the root and Lydian color but does not preserve the full maj7‚ôØ11 identity.

0-1-5-6-10_b6.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-5-6-10_b6.note: G‚ô≠maj7‚ôØ11 is preferred because it is the complete root-position Lydian major-seventh sonority. Music21 agrees semantically. Tonal's F‚ôØmaj‚ôØ4 is an enharmonic Lydian-major label, but its normalized structure omits the major seventh; ChoCo also contains maj7‚ôØ11 observations, so retaining the seventh is musically justified.

0-1-5-6-10_b10.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-5-6-10_b10.note: G‚ô≠maj7‚ôØ11/B‚ô≠ is preferred because B‚ô≠ is the third of a complete G‚ô≠maj7‚ôØ11 voicing. Music21 agrees semantically. Tonal's F‚ôØmaj‚ôØ4/B‚ô≠ gives the Lydian color but drops the major seventh from the comparable structure, so it is not a better musician-facing answer.

0-1-5-7-10_b5.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-5-7-10_b5.note: B‚ô≠m6/9/F is preferred because it is a complete minor-six-nine chord with its fifth in the bass, a more conventional inversion than the complete Gm7‚ô≠5(add11)/F reading with its flat seventh in the bass. Music21 chooses the valid G-rooted half-diminished interpretation, while tonal includes B‚ô≠m6/9/F. Related root-bass cases favor their respective structures, and key context does not resolve this F-bass ambiguity.

0-1-6-8-10_b0.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-6-8-10_b0.note: B‚ô≠m7‚ôØ5/C is preferred because the voicing contains the complete B‚ô≠ minor-seventh-sharp-five shell B‚ô≠-D‚ô≠-F‚ôØ-A‚ô≠, with the slash-bass C supplying the ninth color. Tonal agrees semantically as B‚ô≠m9‚ôØ5/C. Music21's G‚ô≠/C add A‚ô≠,B‚ôØ captures a related major-shell subset, but it drops the defining B‚ô≠ minor seventh and sharp-fifth organization.

0-1-6-8-10_b1.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-6-8-10_b1.note: B‚ô≠m9‚ôØ5/D‚ô≠ is preferred because the voicing contains the complete B‚ô≠ minor-seventh-sharp-five shell B‚ô≠-D‚ô≠-F‚ôØ-A‚ô≠ with C as the ninth, and D‚ô≠ is the minor third in the bass. Tonal agrees. Music21's F‚ôØ/C‚ôØ add G‚ôØ,B‚ôØ captures an upper-structure major shell but drops the defining minor seventh and sharp-fifth quality.

0-1-6-8-10_b8.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-6-8-10_b8.note: B‚ô≠m9‚ôØ5/A‚ô≠ is preferred because A‚ô≠ is the flat seventh of a complete B‚ô≠m9‚ôØ5 sonority. Tonal agrees. Music21's F‚ôØ/G‚ôØ add B‚ôØ is a partial major/add-tone reading of the same pitch classes, but it omits the B‚ô≠-D‚ô≠-F‚ôØ-A‚ô≠ minor-seventh-sharp-five structure.

0-1-6-8-10_b10.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-6-8-10_b10.note: B‚ô≠m9‚ôØ5 is preferred because it is root position and names every tone as part of a complete minor-seventh-sharp-five ninth chord. Tonal agrees. Music21's G‚ô≠/B‚ô≠ add A‚ô≠,C is a related major-shell bookkeeping label, but it does not preserve the complete B‚ô≠ minor seventh quality.

0-1-6-9_b6.label: clearly-correct
0-1-6-9_b6.note: F‚ôØm‚ôØ11 is the clearest isolated-voicing name because F‚ôØ is the bass and root of a complete minor triad, with C/B‚ôØ supplying a single sharp-eleventh color. Music21's F‚ôØdimaddC‚ôØ requires contradictory flat-five and perfect-fifth roles, while tonal's D‚ô≠maj7‚ôØ5sus4/F‚ôØ is a highly altered suspended slash interpretation. Key context does not displace the structurally direct F‚ôØ-rooted reading.

0-1-6-9_b9.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-6-9_b9.note: A6add‚ôØ9 is preferred because A is the bass/root and the voicing contains C‚ôØ/D‚ô≠ as the major third, F‚ôØ as the sixth, and C/B‚ôØ as sharp-nine split-third color. F‚ôØm‚ôØ11/A is a strong alternate because it preserves a complete F‚ôØ minor triad with A in the bass, while music21 and tonal choose related diminished or suspended augmented-major spellings. Without key context, the split-third sixth chord and the upper-structure minor reading remain genuinely ambiguous.

0-2-4-8_b2.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-2-4-8_b2.note: E7#5/D is correct; the notes are the augmented triad C-E-G# plus D, and E is the only augmented-equivalent root whose b7 (D) is present, making E7#5 the unique complete four-note reading with the bass as a chord tone (third inversion). Caug(add9)/D (music21, tonal #1) is the salient triad-first reading but strands the bass D as the added 9th. tonal corroborates E7#5/D at #2. Mirrors the b0 sibling, where C-in-bass correctly prefers root-position Caugadd9: an aug-member bass favors the augmented reading, a non-member bass favors the complete dominant whose bass is a core tone. Sound; no engine change needed.

0-2-4-6-8-10_b0.label: oracle-limitation
0-2-4-6-8-10_b0.note: C9#5#11 is preferred because this is the complete whole-tone collection and C is the bass/root, so C-D-E-F#-G#/Ab-Bb reads directly as a root-position dominant 9#5#11. Tonal agrees and lists the other whole-tone re-roots as alternates. Music21's Bb9#5/C add E keeps the same symmetric material but chooses a non-bass root and demotes the #11 to an added tone.

0-2-4-6-8-10_b2.label: oracle-limitation
0-2-4-6-8-10_b2.note: D9#5#11 is preferred because the whole-tone collection is symmetric and D is the bass/root, yielding a complete root-position dominant 9#5#11. Tonal agrees and lists the other whole-tone re-roots as alternates. Music21's C9#5/D add F# chooses a different symmetric root and treats the bass-rooted #11 as an add tone.

0-2-4-6-8-10_b6.label: oracle-limitation
0-2-4-6-8-10_b6.note: Gb9#5#11 is preferred because Gb/F# is the bass/root of a complete whole-tone dominant 9#5#11 spelling. Tonal agrees semantically as F#9#5#11 and lists the other symmetric re-roots as alternates. Music21's E9#5/F# add A# is a related whole-tone re-rooting but less direct for a context-free bass-rooted chord label.

0-2-4-9_b0.label: context-dependent
0-2-4-9_b0.note: C6/9 is preferred in C major because C is the bass/root and E, A, and D supply the major third, sixth, and ninth of a tonic major-six-nine sonority. In A minor, music21's Am/C add D interpretation becomes the more contextually natural reading because the same notes outline a first-inversion A minor add-eleven chord. This row is context-dependent rather than a fixed oracle disagreement.

0-2-4-9_b2.label: oracle-limitation
0-2-4-9_b2.note: D7sus2 is a complete root-position reading that explains every tone without alterations; D9(no3) is a more traditional name for the same voicing, but omission notation is outside the current vocabulary; music21's Am/D is a valid upper-structure description, while tonal's E-root altered sus reading is less direct; ChoCo has no dominant7sus2 observations

0-2-5-8_b8.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-2-5-8_b8.note: Fm6/A‚ô≠ is preferred because F-A‚ô≠-C-D forms a complete F minor-sixth chord with the minor third in the bass, a conventional inversion. Dm7‚ô≠5/A‚ô≠ is the equally complete enharmonic half-diminished reading with the flat fifth in the bass, and music21, tonal, and pychord all expose that alternate. The more conventional bass role favors Fm6/A‚ô≠, but the half-diminished reinterpretation makes the ambiguity genuine.

0-2-5-7_b0.label: clearly-correct
0-2-5-7_b0.note: Csus2sus4 is correct; it is the complete root-position double-suspension template and scores clearly above the incomplete or slash alternatives. Tonal's Csus24 and pychord's Csus4add2 are semantically equivalent names for the same C-rooted structure.

0-2-5-7_b2.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-2-5-7_b2.note: Dm7(add11) is preferred with D in the bass because D, F, C, and G supply the root, minor third, flat seventh, and eleventh of a conventional root-position minor-eleventh shell with an omitted fifth. Tonal's D-rooted first choice supports only the root hearing, not the full minor-eleventh label. G7sus4/D and Csus2sus4/D are complete and valid slash-chord alternatives, so the ambiguity is genuine, but their roots do not align with the bass.

0-1-3-6-8-10_b0.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-3-6-8-10_b0.note: D‚ô≠maj13sus4/C is preferred because the voicing forms a complete D‚ô≠ suspended-major thirteenth stack with C as the major seventh in the bass. Tonal's G‚ô≠6/9‚ôØ11/C names the same six-note Lydian collection from the subdominant side, while music21's A‚ô≠11/C is also structurally complete. The D‚ô≠ reading is bass-role driven, but all three roots describe plausible organizations of the same quartal/Lydian material.

0-1-3-6-8-10_b1.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-3-6-8-10_b1.note: G‚ô≠6/9‚ôØ11/D‚ô≠ is preferred because G‚ô≠-B‚ô≠-D‚ô≠-E‚ô≠-A‚ô≠-C forms a complete Lydian major-six-nine sonority with D‚ô≠ as the fifth in the bass. Tonal agrees on the F‚ôØ/G‚ô≠ 6/9‚ôØ11 identity, while music21's A‚ô≠11/D‚ô≠ and the near-tie D‚ô≠maj13sus4 reading give complete alternatives. The G‚ô≠ label is clearer as a musician-facing symbol, but the competing roots are musically real.

0-1-3-6-8-10_b3.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-6-8-10_b3.note: G‚ô≠6/9‚ôØ11/E‚ô≠ is preferred because G‚ô≠-B‚ô≠-D‚ô≠-E‚ô≠-A‚ô≠-C forms a complete Lydian major six-nine sonority, with E‚ô≠ as the sixth in the bass. Tonal agrees on the F‚ôØ/G‚ô≠ 6/9‚ôØ11 identity. Music21's A‚ô≠11/E‚ô≠ is a complete dominant-eleventh re-rooting, but it creates the major-third-against-eleventh clash that the G‚ô≠ Lydian label avoids.

0-1-3-6-8-10_b6.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-6-8-10_b6.note: G‚ô≠6/9‚ôØ11 is preferred because the notes are degrees 1-6 of G‚ô≠ Lydian over a root-position G‚ô≠ major triad, the canonical major six-nine sharp-eleven sound. Tonal agrees on the F‚ôØ/G‚ô≠ 6/9‚ôØ11 identity. Music21's A‚ô≠11/G‚ô≠ stacks thirds off the flat seventh into a complete dominant eleventh whose major-third-against-eleventh clash is exactly the dissonance the G‚ô≠ Lydian label avoids.

0-1-3-6-8-10_b8.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-6-8-10_b8.note: A‚ô≠11 is preferred because A‚ô≠ is the bass/root and the voicing supplies a complete dominant-eleventh stack A‚ô≠-C-E‚ô≠-G‚ô≠-B‚ô≠-D‚ô≠. Music21 agrees. Tonal's G‚ô≠6/9‚ôØ11/A‚ô≠ re-roots the same collection as Lydian major-six-nine color, but it turns the bass into the ninth; ChoCo also favors the dominant-eleventh vocabulary here, with 388 dominant7|nine,eleven observations versus 5 exact major6|sharp11,add9 observations.

0-1-3-5-6-8-10_b8.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-3-5-6-8-10_b8.note: A‚ô≠13 is preferred because A‚ô≠ is the bass/root and the voicing supplies the complete dominant-thirteenth stack A‚ô≠-C-E‚ô≠-G‚ô≠-B‚ô≠-D‚ô≠-F. Music21 agrees semantically as G‚ôØ13. Tonal's G‚ô≠maj13‚ôØ11/A‚ô≠ is a valid Lydian-major re-rooting of the same collection, and WhatChord surfaces it as a close alternate; however, it makes the bass the ninth while the A‚ô≠ label keeps root-position dominant evidence. ChoCo also favors the broad dominant-thirteenth vocabulary, with 8947 dominant7|nine,eleven,thirteen observations versus 29 major7|nine,sharp11,thirteen observations.

0-1-3-6-8-10_b10.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-3-6-8-10_b10.note: G‚ô≠6/9‚ôØ11/B‚ô≠ is preferred because B‚ô≠ is the major third of the complete G‚ô≠ Lydian major-six-nine sonority. Tonal lists both B‚ô≠m11(no5) and F‚ôØ6/9‚ôØ11/B‚ô≠, while music21 chooses an A‚ô≠11/B‚ô≠ re-rooting. The B‚ô≠ bass makes the minor-eleventh view tempting, but the G‚ô≠ label names all six tones as a coherent major-family color without treating G‚ô≠/F‚ôØ as an altered minor fifth.
